challenged_assumptions: A2 survives: the response defends generated TypeScript coupling rather than reducing it. `registry.generated.ts` remains the source of `AppThemeName`, `APP_THEMES`, and `THEME_FONTS` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19,39), replacing today’s local global type union (app/src/types/index.ts:8). A stale or hand-authored registry can still type-check while getting order/fonts wrong.

A3 survives in revised form: the response relies on a drift test instead of generating on the normal unit-test path (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:40,62), but `make test-unit` and `npm run test:unit` still run Vitest directly (Makefile:107-109; app/package.json:10). The drift test only covers folder/key mismatch; it does not catch stale generated `THEME_FONTS` or stale sort order after editing an existing manifest’s `fontLoad` or `order`.

A4 survives as an evolution constraint: the response is correct that current banners can be copied as static strings, but that defense depends on duplicating embedded colors from the current SVGs (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:181-291) and handcrafted backgrounds (app/src/lib/theme.ts:376-581). Function-free manifests remain brittle for token-derived banners/patterns despite G2/G5 theme-author self-service (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:21-26).

A5 survives against the composability claim: parity for the five existing themes can be forced by transcription tests, but a single `ElevationName` still couples card, button, nav, and surface props (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:16,45). The current implementation has separate card/button/nav/content-panel switches (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:51-176), so future mixed combinations require raw overrides or shared-code edits.

A6 is resolved only for the duplicate `fun` source of truth. The revised hardcoded allowlist `['hardDrop','pixelBevel','floralGlow']` in `useThemeStyles` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:14,43) recreates a consumer-side look list equivalent to today’s `toy|pixel|floral` rule (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:345-346). It assumes elevation identity implies theme mood, which is not true for reusable treatments.

A7 referential stability is largely resolved by caching, and eager construction is not worse than today’s five top-level `createTheme()` calls (app/src/lib/theme.ts:206,287,379,476,583). The remaining risk is different: if `themes/index.ts` eagerly builds all cached Chakra themes at import time (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:9,44), manifest-only callers of the public theme API still pull `extendTheme` side effects and lose tree-shaking potential.
paradigm_risks: The revised design still says treatments are fully composable (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:52-53), but `elevation` is a bundled card/button/nav/surface treatment (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:16,45). That is a renamed closed look package, not independent treatment composition.

G2 says adding a theme is dropping a folder with no shared-code edits (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:21-23), but the revised `isFunTheme` allowlist and finite elevation/icon/pattern unions mean some valid theme looks still require consumer or treatment-library changes.

Making `order:number` mandatory moves UI placement into each independently authored folder (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,10). That preserves current picker order only if authors coordinate globally, which conflicts with the drop-in-folder authoring story.
replaceability_gaps: `registry.generated.ts` is not replaceable glue because it owns runtime lookup, type derivation, and font metadata (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19,39). The new story-2 hand stub increases this gap: consumers can compile against a manual approximation before the generator exists (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,49-50).

`contentSurface` and `colorScheme` now describe overlapping rendering facts. The response says `colorScheme` describes appBg lightness and does not derive `contentSurface` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:18-20), while current Minecraft needs a light panel because `appBg` is dark and `textStrong` is dark (app/src/lib/theme.ts:538-545,718). Coherence must be validated from actual colors, not trusted booleans.

`ElevationTreatment` cannot be replaced independently from `useThemeStyles` because it must reproduce exact BoxProps currently owned by separate functions (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:51-176,325-349). A treatment table change can break layout consumers without changing their code.

Function-free manifests are replaceable only if raw strings stay literal. Banner SVGs currently embed theme-specific color choices (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:181-291), so future token changes can leave copied banner colors stale unless a validator or generator understands the SVG content.
seam_risks: Stub/generator divergence is not detected by the proposed `prebuild` as written. `node scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs && vitest ...` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:16) rewrites the committed file and then tests the rewritten output; it does not fail on a diff against the story-2 stub unless a separate `--check` mode or `git diff --exit-code` gate exists. This weakens AC2 idempotence (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:144-145).

A literal `readdirSync('../../src/themes')` in a Vitest test under `app/tests/unit` is path-wrong: filesystem relatives resolve from process cwd, and the project runs Vitest from `app` (Makefile:109; app/package.json:10). The `@` alias in `app/vitest.config.ts:5-11` only affects imports, not Node `fs` paths.

The proposed `readdirSync(src/themes).filter(isDir)` drift test (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:13) will exclude `README.md`, `index.ts`, `schema.ts`, and `registry.generated.ts`, but it will include `treatments/`, which is explicitly a directory under `src/themes` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:63-78). It must filter theme folders by `manifest.ts` or an explicit exclusion list.

The drift test compares registry keys to folder names, so it misses generated metadata drift for existing folders: changing `order` or `fontLoad` in a manifest can require regenerating sorted `APP_THEMES` or `THEME_FONTS` while the folder set remains unchanged (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,13,31).

`order:number` has no stated uniqueness validation. Profile order is user-visible because it currently renders `Object.values(APP_THEMES)` (app/pages/profile.tsx:220-241), and the current insertion order is explicit in `APP_THEMES` (app/src/lib/theme.ts:677-733). Duplicate orders can still be deterministic in JS but semantically ambiguous, and gaps may or may not be acceptable; the revised proposal does not say.

`isFunTheme` derived from elevation couples consumer behavior to treatment names. Two themes can legitimately share `hardDrop`, or a fun theme can use `softDrop`; the response’s allowlist (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:14) would misclassify both without a manifest field or separate semantic treatment.

`contentSurface:true` with `colorScheme:'light'`, or `contentSurface:false` with dark appBg and failing `textStrong` contrast, are possible unless validation checks effective rendered pairs. The response mentions WCAG and dark-appBg legibility (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:13) but does not define the actual invariant across `appBg`, `surfaceBg`, `textStrong`, `colorScheme`, and `contentSurface`.
parallel_hazards: Story 2 creates a committed hand-authored `registry.generated.ts` stub and story 3 replaces it with a generator (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,49-50). Any branch adding a theme during that window can update the stub in a shape the later generator does not reproduce.

Because `order` is manually assigned per theme folder, parallel theme branches can both choose the same order. The generator can sort them, but without a uniqueness check the merge conflict becomes a runtime/UI-order ambiguity instead of a validation failure.

The generated registry is a shared write target for story 2 manifests, story 3 codegen, story 4 consumers, and future theme additions. Since it exports both types and runtime data (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:19), stale generated output can create misleading type safety across branches.

Running the generator in `prebuild` mutates source before tests (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:16). Without a check-only mode, CI can pass while leaving uncommitted generated changes locally; without generation on `test:unit`, developers can test against stale committed output.
evolution_triggers: A new theme needing pixel-like cards but a flat nav, or floral buttons with a soft card, will force splitting `elevation` into independent `card/button/nav` selectors or relying on raw overrides. Current behavior already has independent switch bodies (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:51-176).

A new fun theme that reuses `flat` or `softDrop`, or a non-fun theme that reuses `hardDrop`, will expose the hardcoded `isFunTheme` allowlist as non-pluggable (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:14).

A theme author who changes an existing theme’s font metadata or order will need registry regeneration even though the folder drift test passes. That is a normal maintenance path, not an edge case, because `_document` must derive the Google Fonts link from `THEME_FONTS` (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:116-118,172-173).

A true dark theme with light text and no panel should be valid, while Minecraft’s dark appBg with dark text requires a panel (app/src/lib/theme.ts:538-545,718). This will force the validator to stop treating `colorScheme` or `contentSurface` as a shortcut and validate actual contrast pairs.

A token-derived banner or pattern will break the function-free manifest boundary. The response explicitly defers token-derived treatments to the future (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:41), but current SVG/background assets already duplicate token-like colors (app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts:181-291; app/src/lib/theme.ts:376-581).
blocking_concerns: Define a check-only generator/idempotence gate before implementation starts. Story 3 must fail if generated `registry.generated.ts` differs from the committed story-2 stub; merely running the generator before Vitest is not sufficient for AC2 (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:144-145; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,16).

Fix the proposed drift test design before relying on it: resolve `src/themes` relative to the test module or project root explicitly, and classify theme folders by presence of `manifest.ts` so `treatments/` is not counted (app/vitest.config.ts:5-11; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:63-78; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:13).

Add validation for generated metadata freshness beyond folder keys: recompute and compare order and `THEME_FONTS`, or run the generator in check mode on unit-test/CI paths. Folder drift alone will not catch existing-manifest changes (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:8,13,31).

Specify and enforce `order` invariants: unique positive integers at minimum, and whether gaps are allowed. This is required because profile iteration order is visible UI behavior (app/pages/profile.tsx:220-241) and current parity depends on the existing `APP_THEMES` order (app/src/lib/theme.ts:677-733).

Specify the `contentSurface` coherence invariant in terms of actual contrast pairs. The implementation needs a clear rule for `colorScheme`, `appBg`, `surfaceBg`, `textStrong`, and `contentSurface`; otherwise contradictory manifests can pass zod while failing the Minecraft contrast class of bug (app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts:6-18; app/src/lib/theme.ts:538-545,718).

Decide whether `isFunTheme` is a semantic manifest field, a treatment capability, or legacy-only derived output. The revised elevation allowlist is still shared-code coupling and can violate G2 for future themes (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:21-23; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-r1-response.md:14).

Either split `elevation` into independent card/button/nav selectors or explicitly document that mixed combinations require `overrides` and add validation/parity coverage for those overrides. As written, the single elevation token does not fully satisfy the stated composable-treatment decision (specs/epic-pluggable-themes/spec.md:52-53; specs/epic-pluggable-themes/arch-debate-proposal.md:16,45).
